title: Access Tuple Items
content:
### Accessing Tuples

Accessing tuple items works the **same** as lists.

---

### Indexing

```python
fruits = ("apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange", "kiwi")

print(fruits[0])     # "apple"
print(fruits[2])     # "cherry"
print(fruits[-1])    # "kiwi" (last item)
print(fruits[-2])    # "orange"
```

---

### Slicing

```python
fruits = ("apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange", "kiwi")

print(fruits[1:3])   # ("banana", "cherry")
print(fruits[:3])    # ("apple", "banana", "cherry")
print(fruits[2:])    # ("cherry", "orange", "kiwi")
print(fruits[-3:-1]) # ("cherry", "orange")
```

---

### Check if Item Exists

```python
fruits = ("apple", "banana", "cherry")

if "banana" in fruits:
    print("Yes, banana is in the tuple")
```

---

> **Note:** Since tuples are immutable, you cannot change items after creation. Access methods work the same as lists.
codeExample:
fruits = ("apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange", "kiwi")

print("First:", fruits[0])
print("Last:", fruits[-1])
print("Slice:", fruits[1:4])

if "cherry" in fruits:
    print("cherry is in the tuple")

# Loop through
for fruit in fruits:
    print(fruit)

quiz.question: Can you change an item in a tuple after it is created?
options: Only if you convert to list first
